question: Which Kubernetes autoscaler changes the number of Pod replicas based on metric targets (CPU, memory, custom)?
explanation: HPA scales replica count up or down based on observed metrics vs. target thresholds.

question: Which autoscaler adjusts the CPU and memory requests/limits of individual Pods over time?
explanation: VPA recommends and optionally applies updated resource requests/limits — scaling each Pod up instead of adding replicas.

question: Which autoscaler changes the number of Nodes in the cluster in response to pending (unschedulable) Pods?
explanation: Cluster Autoscaler adds Nodes when Pods are Pending due to insufficient capacity and removes under-utilized Nodes.

question: Which CNCF-incubating Node autoscaler uses just-in-time provisioning with instance-type flexibility (originally on AWS, now multi-cloud)?
explanation: Karpenter picks instance types dynamically based on pending Pod requirements, often improving speed and bin-packing vs. classic Cluster Autoscaler.

question: Which CNCF project enables event-driven autoscaling of workloads based on external signals (e.g., Kafka lag, queue depth)?
explanation: KEDA (Kubernetes Event-Driven Autoscaling) wraps HPA so that scalers can consume external signals like queue depth, database offsets, or Prometheus queries.

question: A team uses HPA on a Deployment but wants to scale from zero when traffic arrives. What do they typically need?

options.label: a
options.text: KEDA or Knative, because plain HPA requires at least one replica

explanation: Plain HPA requires at least one replica. KEDA and Knative enable scale-to-zero via event-driven and request-driven activation.

question: Why can HPA and VPA conflict if both are applied to the same workload on CPU?

options.label: a
options.text: HPA scales replicas up when CPU rises; VPA may simultaneously raise CPU requests per Pod, changing the baseline

options.label: b
options.text: They cannot coexist at all under any circumstance

options.label: d
options.text: VPA replaces HPA by disabling it

explanation: Using both on the same metric axis can thrash. Either run them on different metrics (e.g., VPA on memory, HPA on CPU) or run VPA in recommend-only mode.

question: Which Kubernetes API does HPA query by default to retrieve CPU/memory metrics?
explanation: metrics-server implements the resource metrics API (metrics.k8s.io) that HPA consumes for CPU and memory.
